reason: Invariant preservation goal reduces to proving 8 <= j from the other invariants (notably 7 <= j). That is not derivable, so the invariant set is inconsistent/non-inductive across the loop body (after i := i+2; j := j-1 the relation i <= j+2 tends to force j to stay >= 8, which is false at loop exit where j==6). This points to an overly-strong/incorrect loop invariant rather than a missing bridge fact.
